skill.name: viral-generator-builder
skill.description: Guides the design of shareable generator tools, including name generators, quizzes, avatar creators, personality tests, and calculators.
skill.summary: Builds practical plans for viral generator tools with shareable results.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found no executable command usage or cryptographic implementation. The flagged backticks are Markdown code fences, and the skill is instructional content about generator design. No prompt injection, network access, filesystem access, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The scanner interpreted Markdown code fences as Ruby shell backtick execution. The surrounding content is documentation and JavaScript-style examples, not executable Ruby or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens are visible as Markdown fences and template literals inside documentation. No command invocation, Ruby code, or shell execution path appears in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography at several lines, but those locations contain prose, headings, tables, or result-design guidance. No evidence found for a cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive hashing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the reported lines found no MD5, SHA-1, encryption, password storage, or security-sensitive digest use. The only hash discussion is a non-security deterministic generator idea.

content.value_statement: Many generator ideas fail because results are generic and hard to share. This skill helps plan low-friction tools with memorable results, quiz logic, and social sharing mechanics.
actual_capabilities: Plans generator tool flows from minimal input to shareable results.
Designs quiz structures with weighted scoring and result categories.
Suggests name generator patterns, including deterministic and seeded approaches.
Improves result pages with screenshot-ready cards and share calls to action.
Identifies anti-patterns that reduce sharing, such as long forms and bland results.
limitations: Does not provide production-ready application code by itself.
Does not validate market demand or guarantee viral growth.
Does not include analytics, hosting, moderation, or privacy implementation details.
Image and avatar generation still require separate design or model tooling.

use_cases.title: Launch a Name Generator
use_cases.description: Plan a low-friction name generator with repeatable outputs, themed word lists, and a result page users can share.

use_cases.title: Create a Personality Quiz
use_cases.description: Turn a campaign idea into quiz questions, weighted scoring, result categories, and share prompts.

use_cases.title: Improve a Share Card
use_cases.description: Review a generator result page and make the card clearer, more personal, and easier to screenshot.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Simple Generator
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a new tool idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a name generator for [audience]. Include inputs, output style, result copy, and share card ideas.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Quiz Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for a personality test.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a personality quiz about [topic]. Include questions, scoring logic, result types, and retake behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize for Sharing
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for improving an existing tool.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my generator concept: [concept]. Suggest changes that improve screenshots, result identity, and social sharing.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Launch Spec
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for a complete product plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a launch-ready spec for [generator idea]. Include user flow, scoring, result taxonomy, share mechanics, and risks.

output_examples.input: Plan a fantasy name generator for tabletop players.
output_examples.output: A concise product plan with one name input, themed name pools, deterministic results, result meanings, and a card designed for sharing.

output_examples.input: Design a quiz that tells founders their startup archetype.
output_examples.output: A quiz outline with six questions, four result categories, weighted scoring guidance, result descriptions, and share prompts.

output_examples.input: Improve this result page so people share it more often.
output_examples.output: A prioritized review covering result specificity, mobile readability, card hierarchy, branding, and calls to compare with friends.

best_practices: Start with the smallest input that still creates a personal result.
Make every result specific, flattering, and visually clear on mobile.
Design the result card before building the rest of the flow.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for long forms before users see value.
Do not create generic results that could describe anyone.
Do not hide the result behind cluttered sharing or signup steps.

faq.question: What kinds of generators does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports name generators, quizzes, personality tests, avatar concepts, text generators, and shareable calculators.

faq.question: Can it build the full application?
faq.answer: It mainly guides product structure, result design, and viral mechanics. Implementation still requires normal development work.

faq.question: Does it guarantee viral growth?
faq.answer: No. It improves shareability patterns, but distribution, timing, audience fit, and quality still matter.

faq.question: Can it help with quiz scoring?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline weighted scoring, result categories, and simple logic for matching answers to results.

faq.question: Is it useful for marketing campaigns?
faq.answer: Yes. It can shape campaign tools that produce personal, branded, and screenshot-ready outputs.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
